name.text: Grouping Raw Data into a Frequency Distribution Table (Milk Production)

questiontext.text:
A dairy farm recorded the amount of milk (in liters) produced by its cows during the month of January. The milk production values for {@n@} cows are: \[ {@data@} \] Group the data into the following frequency distribution table.
Milk Produced (liters) Frequency
200–239 [[input:ans1]] [[validation:ans1]]
240–279 [[input:ans2]] [[validation:ans2]]
280–319 [[input:ans3]] [[validation:ans3]]
320–359 [[input:ans4]] [[validation:ans4]]

generalfeedback.text:
The raw data represent the milk production (in liters) of {@n@} cows during January.
\[ {@data@} \]
We group the values into the given intervals.
### Interval 200–239
Values in this range:
\[ {sublist(data,lambda([x], x>=200 and x<=239))} \]
Frequency:
\[ {@f1@} \]
### Interval 240–279
Values in this range:
\[ {sublist(data,lambda([x], x>=240 and x<=279))} \]
Frequency:
\[ {@f2@} \]
### Interval 280–319
Values in this range:
\[ {sublist(data,lambda([x], x>=280 and x<=319))} \]
Frequency:
\[ {@f3@} \]
### Interval 320–359
Values in this range:
\[ {sublist(data,lambda([x], x>=320 and x<=359))} \]
Frequency:
\[ {@f4@} \]
The frequencies add up to the total number of cows:
\[ {@f1@}+{@f2@}+{@f3@}+{@f4@} = {@n@} \]
Thus the completed frequency distribution table is correct.

questionnote.text:
This question assesses students' ability to group raw data into a frequency distribution table.
Randomisation:
* The number of cows `n` is randomly generated between 12 and 18. * A list `data` contains randomly generated milk production values between 200 and 359 liters. * Students must classify the values into four class intervals:
* 200–239 * 240–279 * 280–319 * 320–359
Frequencies are automatically computed using Maxima by counting how many values in the list fall within each interval.
Teacher answers: ans1 = f1 ans2 = f2 ans3 = f3 ans4 = f4
The sum of all frequencies equals the number of cows `n`.

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.
